Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Preliminary Injunction Against Defendants Wilmington Trust, National Association As Trustee Of The Mfra Trust 2014-2 And Fay Servicing Llc
Off Calendar Mar-27-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nelson Goodell (415-495-3950) for Plaintiff/ opposing party via CourtCall.
Lukasz Wozniak (949-477-5069) for Defendant/Moving party via CourtCall.
Deborah Fuqua CSR #12948, Dfuquacsr@gmail.com, 415-810-7170, Court reporter.

The Court announces the tentative ruling as revised, off the record.

Hearing is held. Argued re: attorney's fees, contract interpretation, bond amount, competent evidence, sanctions, lack of standing. The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JULIANA CLEGG's Motion For A Preliminary Injunction Against Defendants Wilmington Trust, National Association As Trustee Of The Mfra Trust 2014-2 And Fay Servicing Llc is GRANTED. The Court recognizes the authority provided in Hart and Chacker; and that in this case the amount and reasonableness of the fees to be added to the debt is in dispute, unlike in Hart and Chacker. Defendant to release the lien within 24 business hours (arrangements to be made with the escrow company, North American Title Company) of Plaintiff (1) paying the amounts demanded (except for attorney's fees) to pay off the loan; (2) Plaintiff depositing in an escrow account the amount of paid attorney's fees, $141,083.36; and (3) Plaintiff posting a bond with the Court in the amount of $50,000. Plaintiff shall make the payments, deposits and postings referenced above (in any sequence that is practicable for escrow to timely close) by no later than April 3, 2020. If plaintiff fails to comply, the injunction is automatically dissolved. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).
Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Deborah Fuqua CSR #12948, Dfuquacsr@gmail.com, 415-810-7170, Court reporter. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FAY SERVICING, LLC and WILMINGTON TRUST's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is denied as to the request for punitive damages. Sufficient facts are alleged to support a request for punitive damages based on fraud. Motion is GRANTED as to the request and references to emotional distress damages. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for a proposition that at trust, as opposed to an individual, may recover emotional distress damages. Green v. Central Mortgage (2015) 148 F. Supp.3d 852 is not on point. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
